At this stage, babies exhibit sufficient physical maturity necessary for successful surgical interventions yet remain young enough so that any required follow-up procedures can also be managed more effectively compared to waiting till much older such as 5 years where additional complexities might emerge including possible maladaptive behaviors related to prolonged disfigurement issues unaddressed earlier.
